title: Extract shared deterministic materializeContracts helper
description: Pull the contract-creation loop at src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:861-906 into a reusable materializeContracts(spec, projectRoot, config) helper that runTsPipeline calls in place of the inline loop. Feature-derived contract content is preserved; contract ids become deterministic and zero-padded as sprint-<specId>-NN so listContracts lexical ordering matches execution order. The post-plan and post-sprint-contract audit checkpoints stay in the pipeline.

successCriteria.description: Running npm run build and the TypeScript typecheck completes with zero errors after the helper extraction.
A unit test confirms runTsPipeline delegates contract creation to materializeContracts while the post-plan and post-sprint-contract audit checkpoints remain in the pipeline body.

With generateContractPrecision mocked, a three-feature spec that has no embedded sprints produces feature-derived contracts whose title, description, successCriteria, status, precision fields, sprintNumber and features match the pre-refactor output.
Materialized contract ids follow the deterministic sprint-<specId>-NN form, zero-padded so that listContracts lexical ordering matches sprint execution order for a spec with twelve or more sprints.
The full existing vitest suite passes with no regressions in run.test.ts after the refactor.

nonGoals: Do not change the pipeline phase order, audit checkpoint sites, or the research and plan steps.
Do not introduce the embedded spec.sprints branch in this sprint; that arrives in Sprint 2.
stopConditions: Stop when materializeContracts is the single source of feature-derived contract creation called by runTsPipeline and the suite is green.
Stop when contract ids are deterministic and zero-padded and ordering is verified for twelve-plus sprints.
definitionOfDone: The contract-creation loop is extracted into a reusable materializeContracts helper that runTsPipeline calls; feature-derived contract content is preserved, contract ids are deterministic and zero-padded for stable ordering, and the full test suite passes.
assumptions: generateContractPrecision remains an async planner LLM call and is mocked in tests.
The post-plan and post-sprint-contract checkpoints are pipeline concerns and stay outside the helper.
generatorNotes: Keep the feature-derived branch faithful to pipeline.ts:861-906: same createContract inputs (criteria mapped with verificationMethod agent-evaluation), same logging strings, same order. Replace only the id assignment with deterministic zero-padded sprint-<specId>-NN. Helper signature: materializeContracts(spec, projectRoot, config). Have it return the SprintContract[] so the pipeline can pass it to the post-sprint-contract checkpoint. Honor the freeze comment intent (pipeline.ts:610-612) for everything except the now-authorized id scheme and the later embedded-sprints support.
evaluatorNotes: Verify the inline loop is gone from runTsPipeline and replaced by a single helper call. Confirm checkpoints at the post-plan and post-sprint-contract sites still fire with the same arguments. Add or extend a test that builds a 12-sprint spec and asserts listContracts returns contracts in sprintNumber order. Confirm feature-derived content parity by snapshotting contract fields other than id/timestamps.
